questions.questionText: Lithium is the first-line mood stabilizer for bipolar disorder. Its most important side effect requiring monitoring is:
questions.optionB: Nephrogenic diabetes insipidus and hypothyroidism (monitor: serum lithium, renal function, thyroid function — narrow therapeutic index 0.6-1.2 mEq/L)
questions.explanation: Lithium: first-line for bipolar disorder (acute mania and prophylaxis). Narrow therapeutic index: 0.6-1.2 mEq/L (therapeutic), >1.5 (toxic), >2.0 (severe toxicity). Side effects: nephrogenic DI (polyuria, polydipsia — blocked ADH response), hypothyroidism (goiter), tremor, weight gain, acne, GI upset, Ebstein anomaly (teratogenic — avoid in first trimester). Monitoring: serum lithium (regular), renal function (creatinine), thyroid function (TSH), ECG. Toxicity: coarse tremor, ataxia, seizures, renal failure. NSAIDs/thiazides: increase lithium levels (avoid/dose adjust). Reference: Niraj Ahuja — Lithium.
